body.title: CLOSED: Semantic conflict detection in /decided and /audit-ticket
body.result: added memory_search to /decided Step 2.5 (before audit-design) and /audit-ticket Step 0 (semantic duplicate check)
body.description:
The /decided audit step 2.5 'conflicts with last-30d decisions' check is currently manual — reads decisions_log.dsb by eye. /audit-ticket duplicate check similarly requires a grep. Semantic search makes both automatic and comprehensive.

Proposed shape: (a) In /decided Step 2.5 audit, add: call mcp__igor__memory_search(query=decision_summary, limit=5), review results for conflicts before proceeding. (b) In /audit-ticket Step 1, add: call mcp__igor__memory_search(query=ticket_description, limit=5), flag any semantically close prior decisions or tickets found. Both are advisory — results inform judgment, don't block.

**Affected files:** ~/.claude/skills/decided (Step 2.5), ~/.claude/skills/audit-ticket (Step 1)
**Design rules:** none
**Scope boundary:** adds semantic lookup to two existing audit steps. Not replacing grep/log checks — augmenting them.
**Test plan:** file a decision related to D-cc-log-logcontrol-conformance; verify the semantic search surfaces that prior decision during the audit step.
**Pass condition:** /decided on a CC-logging topic surfaces D-cc-log-logcontrol-conformance via semantic match before ticket drafting begins.
